human_verification.notes: Reviewed student affairs execution boundaries, source-class coverage, research-backed lifecycle stages, FERPA and consent handling, campus event and case workflow handling, conduct/Title IX/disability/clinical/safety escalation, study and grant dependencies, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: Scenario coverage validates normal student affairs execution, incomplete mid-process artifacts, completion without evidence, execution exceptions, consent and privacy gaps, formal approval blockers, learner and study workflow handling, terminology normalization, adjacent authority refusals, low-confidence handling, and tenant source conflicts.
Required education-and-research source classes are represented in the research summary.
No blocking unresolved research ambiguities remain for this validated specification-based package.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary decisions separate student affairs execution from conduct adjudication, Title IX outcomes, disability accommodation approval, clinical judgment, safety-critical action, registrar finality, faculty review, grading, instructional judgment, accreditation signoff, grant approval, privacy disclosure, legal interpretation, and research ethics authority.
Research summary covers education and research governance, program and learner workflow references, and records, consent, and assessment handling standards.

Scenario suite covers lifecycle progression, exception queues, completion evidence, missing records, consent and access conflicts, approval blockers, learner and study dependencies, tenant adaptation, and official authority refusals.
Functionality map declares all shared, workflow-phase, and education-and-research abilities with scenario coverage.
notes: The lane remains `specification-based`; retained implementation is not claimed in this pack.

Runtime Promotion to a retained implementation requires explicit governance approval, implementation review, tenant authority-map validation, stable retrieval connectors, sustained operational evidence, and scenario telemetry proving safe escalation behavior.
